---
title: "Session 01 teaching notes: Excel 1"
session_id: session-01
event_id: null
---

## Session 1 — Basics, formulas, charts, and conditional structures

Use [Excelsheet](../materials/introduction.xlsx) (see [solution](../materials/introduction_with_solutions.xlsx))

Anfang: Worksheet (PR)
Excel: Funktionsparametertrenner (komma/semikolon) kann nur Global umgestellt werden, beides zulässig.

Tip: use keyboard instead of clicking (prepare/mention shortcuts)

Relative/absolute references: debug formula (predecessor/successor visualization)

p.12: solution/dataset?

p.17 - test auf trenner: sum(3,5) sollte bei allen 8 geben.

p.17: formula vs function: clarify the difference!

p.20: moving averages: first homework

p.21: how can I open the dialogue?

VIP II: prefer nested ifs

p.25: homework (many fail), end of first session
- Installment Loan an der Tafel durchrechnen; dann: Aufgabe: in Excel übertragen
- Tilgung ab Periode 2: Wenn-funktion (Studierende müssen selbst auf die Wenn funktion kommen)


## Exercises

| Slide(s) | Exercise            | Summary                                                                                                | Files | In-class vs take-home |
| -------: | ------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ----- | --------------------- |
|    7, 10 | Future value        | Build an investment calculator and extend it to compare interest-rate and investment-period scenarios. |       |                       |
|       14 | Coffee-shop charts  | Create two suitable charts showing different aspects of the coffee-sales distribution.                 |       |                       |
|       19 | Sales statistics    | Calculate minimum, maximum, mean, and median sales across customers.                                   |       |                       |
|       20 | DAX moving averages | Calculate 10-day and 20-day moving averages and visualize them using line and scatter charts.          |       |                       |
|       23 | VIP status          | Classify customers as Bronze, Gold, or Platinum based on sales thresholds.                             |       |                       |
|       24 | VIP club credits    | Calculate club-card credits using sales-dependent credit factors.                                      |       |                       |
|       25 | Installment loan    | Create a flexible repayment schedule for an installment loan of up to 20 periods.                      |       |                       |
|       26 | Annuity loan        | Create an annuity-loan repayment schedule using the `PMT` function.                                    |       |                       |
|       27 | Exam result         | Determine whether students passed, failed, or failed because of the quorum requirement.                |       |                       |



<!--
    ## Session overview

    - **Focus:** Excel
    - **Session title:** Excel 1
    - **Coverage target:** TBD

    ## Before class

    - [ ] Review the session slides/materials.
    - [ ] Prepare files, datasets, and examples.
    - [ ] Note any announcements or reminders.

    ## Timing record

    Use this table while preparing or after teaching to record the actual timing.

    | Planned time | Actual time | Topic/activity | Notes for next time |
    |---|---|---|---|
    | 0--10 min |  | Setup / recap |  |
    | 10--30 min |  | Main input |  |
    | 30--60 min |  | Guided practice |  |
    | 60--80 min |  | Independent practice / questions |  |
    | 80--90 min |  | Wrap-up |  |

    ## Notes during class

    - TBD

    ## Follow-up

    - [ ] Update examples or instructions.
    - [ ] Record common questions.
    - [ ] Add reminders for the next session.
-->
